tasks.method: Compute per-match averages for strike rate, economy, and other derived metrics and visualize trends using line charts. Encodings include match id on the x-axis and metric values on the y-axis, with separate lines for batters and bowlers.
tasks.objective: Identify trends and consistency in key metrics for batters and bowlers over matches.

tasks.method: Generate box plots for batters and bowlers to summarize and compare distributions of these statistics. Encodings include role as categorical groups on the x-axis and statistics values plotted as box plot distributions on the y-axis.
tasks.objective: Provide a comparative overview of performance variance and consistency for batters and bowlers.

tasks.method: Use scatter plots to correlate runs scored with balls faced for batters and wickets taken with overs bowled for bowlers. Encodings include one metric on the x-axis and the other on the y-axis, with points colored based on player role.
tasks.objective: Analyze efficiency and consistency in scoring and bowling relative to effort expended.

tasks.method: Use heat maps to show player performance across opposition teams and venues. Encodings include opposition team on the x-axis, venue on the y-axis, and metrics like runs or wickets represented by cell color intensity.
tasks.objective: Identify patterns in player performance based on match context and opposition conditions.

tasks.method: Detect outliers in individual performance using scatter plots or bar charts with sorting. Encodings include player name on the x-axis and metric values (e.g., runs or wickets) on the y-axis, sorted to highlight anomalies.
tasks.objective: Highlight exceptional matches and their impact on overall performance consistency.

tasks.method: Use bar charts to compare total runs, wickets, or averages across seasons and events. Encodings include season or event names on the x-axis and aggregated statistics on the y-axis, with bars representing players' roles or team contributions.
tasks.objective: Assess long-term trends in player and team performances across different seasonal and event contexts.

tasks.type: computing derived values | characterizing distributions | retrieving values
tasks.method: Generate area charts for cumulative runs and boundary metrics. Encodings include match id on the x-axis and cumulative statistics (runs, fours, sixes) on the y-axis, with separate areas for batters and bowlers.
tasks.objective: Evaluate players’ cumulative contribution trends across matches and identify periods of peak performance.

tasks.method: Allow users to select ranges of matches or timelines to sort and view player fantasy points. Encodings include match/season timelines on the x-axis and fantasy points on the y-axis with sorting capabilities.
tasks.objective: Analyze how consistent individual players are in contributing to fantasy metrics, highlighting peak and low periods.

tasks.method: Use clustered heat maps to analyze relationships between toss decisions, winning outcomes, and performance metrics like fours. Encodings include clusters based on toss decisions and match outcomes, with color representing metrics.
tasks.objective: Understand the impact of strategic decisions on match outcomes and player performance metrics in aggregated contexts.

tasks.type: retrieving values | computing derived values | filtering
tasks.method: Use combined charts (bar and line) to compare aggregated performance metrics (e.g., runs and wickets) with averages over time for key players. Encodings include time (seasons/events) on the x-axis and metrics as stacked bars or overlaying lines.
tasks.objective: Analyze aggregated efficiency and contribution trends for both batters and bowlers in a single visualization.
